text.title: Numbering Conventions
text.summary: Standard numbering patterns for features, tasks, requirements, and acceptance criteria used across the PM workflow artifacts.

rule.statement: Use these exact patterns across all PM workflow artifacts: features as {NN}-{name} (e.g., 01-auth), tasks as TASK_{NNN}_* (e.g., TASK_001_setup), functional requirements as FR-{NN}.{N} (e.g., FR-01.1), acceptance criteria as AC-{NN}.{N} (e.g., AC-01.1).
rule.rationale: Consistent numbering enables cross-referencing between specs, task files, and tracker tickets; inconsistent numbering breaks the rice-reorder script, spec-to-task linking, and MLP update mode's in-place edit logic.

rule.statement: Treat .aidocs/features/ as the planning source of truth and Jira/Linear as the execution mirror — never the reverse.
rule.rationale: Tracker tickets get deleted, re-numbered, or migrated; the .aidocs/ file tree is under git and has full history. If the tracker is the source of truth, history is lost when tickets are deleted.

rule.statement: When syncing RICE values to Jira numeric custom fields, coerce explicitly; Jira drops the value silently on type mismatch.
rule.rationale: Jira's REST API does not return an error when a numeric field receives a string value; the value is silently ignored, leaving the field empty in the tracker while appearing correct in the spec frontmatter.

rule.statement: The daily PM orchestrator produces one digest file (.aidocs/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md) and sends only the diff vs. yesterday via tg-send, not the full digest.
rule.rationale: Sending the full digest daily creates Telegram noise; the diff is what the PM needs to act on. Full digests are available on demand from the file.
